rationale: Both correctly connect the failure to PR #4834's new NotImplementedError guard, matching yenuo26's hint, but both overreach past what the thread actually confirms — the thread shows Flink-ddd still asking to trigger a full CI run, not a settled fix, so Y's confident 'keep #4905 closed as resolved' is unsupported and X's hedged 'fix appears already applied... if not, submit a one-line PR' is more epistemically honest. X also lists actual files it read (async_omni.py, test file) while Y invents suspiciously precise details (exact merge dates, line ranges, named regression tests like test_partial_wake_blocks_generate) with no evidence of having read anything, suggesting fabrication. Y is more complete and polished (X's draft is literally cut off mid-sentence), but that completeness is built on less-grounded claims.
